title: How to proceed with diagnosis of brake negative pressure monitoring system failure

blocks.text: How to proceed with diagnosis of brake negative pressure monitoring system failure

items: The HDS (Honda Diagnostics System) must be connected and disconnected with the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode.

blocks.text: 1. Connect the Honda Diagnostics System (HDS) to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. 2. Turn ignition to ON mode. 3. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate data link circuit Inspect the 4. Select EVPS from the brake system menu of HDS and check the DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Code) and freeze data displayed.

items.text: For information on the use of HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual.
HDS can display various PGM-FI ECU data such as freeze data and snapshots, so it is useful to select the necessary mode for diagnostic purposes.

items: The HDS must be connected and disconnected with the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode.

blocks.text: 1. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. 2. Turn ignition to ON mode. 3. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate data link circuit Inspect the 4. Use HDS to clear DTC. For information on the use of HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual.

items: Even after the ignition is turned to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, the PGM-FI ECU is still powered on (for up to 60 minutes) to monitor the vehicle status for a certain period of time. If the PGM-FI ECU coupler is disconnected before the PGM-FI ECU is completely turned off, the PGM-FI ECU may be damaged. This mode can be removed by disconnecting the 12V battery (-) terminal or shorting the SCS line with HDS after the ignition is turned off (LOCK) mode.

blocks.text: 2. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. 3. Turn ignition to ON mode. 4. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate data link circuit Inspect the 5. Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode. 6. Execute SCS short in SCS mode with HDS.
7. Disconnect PGM-FI ECU couplers A (50P) and E (80P).

items.text: If the PGM-FI ECU coupler is disconnected and the ignition is set to ON mode within the fault diagnosis, the PGM-FI warning light may come on, so the DTC should be cleared.

In the inspection performed by disconnecting the coupler of the PGM-FI ECU, the D indicator light may blink because it shares sensors with the CVT system. In this case, clear the DTC after the inspection and confirm that the D indicator light does not blink.

Even after the ignition is turned to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, the PGM-FI ECU is still powered on (for up to 60 minutes) to monitor the vehicle status for a certain period of time. If the PGM-FI ECU coupler is disconnected before the PGM-FI ECU is completely turned off, the PGM-FI ECU may be damaged. This mode can be removed by disconnecting the 12V battery (-) terminal or shorting the SCS line with HDS after the ignition is turned off (LOCK) mode.
For failure diagnosis, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (A) and a test probe (B) with a round tip (recommended commercial tool: Pomona Electronics (product no. 3563)).
To check, insert the test probe into the inspection hole (A) of the coupler.
